title: What is a Database?
description: Discover where apps store all their information.

slides.title: The Brain of an App 🧠
slides.content:
# Apps need a memory!

Think about your favorite social media app. It remembers your profile picture, your friends, and your posts.

But *where* does it store all this? Not on your phone... but somewhere on the internet. That "somewhere" is called a **Database**.

quizData.question: What is the primary purpose of a database?

options.text: To securely store and retrieve information
To make apps run faster on local wifi

quizData.explanation: Databases acts like the memory for applications, securely storing and retrieving all required data.

slides.title: More than just a folder 📂
slides.content:
## Why not just use folders?

You *could* save app data in thousands of text files in a folder. But imagine trying to find one specific user's email out of millions of files.

It would be incredibly slow! 

A database is a **specialized system designed to organize, store, and find data blazingly fast**.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Every time you 'Like' a photo, search for a movie, or buy something online, you are quietly interacting with a database in the background! It's the silent engine powering the web.

quizData.question: Why do apps use databases instead of just thousands of text files?

options.text: Text files cost too much money to create.
Databases are specialized systems designed to organize and find data blazingly fast.
Apple and Google force developers to use databases.

quizData.explanation: Databases are heavily optimized for speed, searching, and managing massive amounts of organized data, unlike plain folders and text files.
